title: Demand Power
howToRead: Read Demand Power as the brand's broad equity pull. It is usually interpreted through Meaningful plus Salient strength, not as a direct causal sales forecast.
healthyPattern: High, ahead, and stable or gaining suggests the brand has strong demand momentum to protect.
cautionPattern: Declining momentum or not-ahead status means the brand may still be large, but its demand advantage needs renewal.

title: Perceived Value
howToRead: Read Perceived Value as whether brand equity helps justify the intended price position. It is broad equity evidence, not SKU-level price guidance. Source lineage may still call this Pricing Power.
healthyPattern: Ahead or category-leading Perceived Value suggests the brand has stronger perceived worth to protect.
cautionPattern: Not-ahead Perceived Value, especially with weak Different or Meaningful, suggests price-value vulnerability.

title: Salient
howToRead: Read Salient as how readily the brand comes to mind in category and occasion contexts.
healthyPattern: High and ahead Salience means memory structures are working and should be protected with consistency.
cautionPattern: Weak or declining Salience suggests the brand may be relevant but not mentally available enough.

title: Meaningful
plainEnglishRole: Relevance and fit with consumer needs.
howToRead: Read Meaningful as whether the brand matters to consumers and fits the jobs or tensions in the category.
healthyPattern: High and ahead Meaningful gives the brand permission to grow, premiumize, or defend relevance.
cautionPattern: Declining or lagging Meaningful suggests the brand proposition, relevance, or delivery should be refreshed.

title: Different
howToRead: Read Different as the brand's distinctiveness and reason to choose. It helps explain both choice power and price justification.
healthyPattern: Ahead or category-leading Difference means the brand has a sharper edge to preserve and translate.
cautionPattern: Not-ahead or lagging Difference can mean the brand is visible but too generic.
